name: infoblox-hosts_get_host_config
description: Fetch the effective DNS or DHCP configuration files for a specific on-prem host. Use for host-specific configuration questions such as DNS zones, forwarders, recursion, DHCP pools, reservations, options, and DDNS behavior. Do not use for general explanations, runtime troubleshooting, or multi-host summaries.

name: infoblox-portal_list_all_available_services
description:
List all available Infoblox Portal API services and their descriptions.

You have access to many services within the Infoblox Portal.
Use this tool to discover what services are available and if a service exists to handle a request.
After calling `list_all_available_services`, `get_service_info` and `get_method_info`
you can call HTTP operations to perform the actions needed.

name: infoblox-portal_get_service_info
description: Discover methods available for a service.

name: infoblox-portal_get_method_info
description: Get detailed parameter requirements for a specific endpoint.

name: infoblox-portal_make_get_request
description:
Execute a GET request to retrieve information and store it for later SQL querying.

The response data will be stored in a session-specific directory and can be queried
using the `query_stored_data` tool with SQL.

Filtering Strategy:
1. Server-side filtering: Use the _filter parameter in query_params for simple
    top-level field filtering. Example: {"_filter": "name == 'John'"}. IMPORTANT: The _filter
    parameter only supports basic expressions on top-level fields and does not handle nested fields.
    For example: {"_filter": "name == 'John' and age > 30"} is supported,
    but {"_filter": "user.name == 'John'"} is not supported.

2. Client-side SQL queries: After calling this tool, use `query_stored_data` to run
    SQL queries against the stored data for:
    - Filtering on nested fields
    - Complex conditions (AND/OR)
    - Joins across multiple GET requests
    - Aggregations (COUNT, AVG, SUM, etc.)
    - Analysis of results

Returns:
    The API response data and the table name for SQL querying.

name: infoblox-portal_make_post_request
description:
Execute a POST request to create new objects or perform complex analysis.

Calling this tool will surface a UI confirmation form for the user with the ability
to edit fields before any changes are committed. Call this tool directly when the user asks to update
or modify something — you do not need to ask for permission or clarification first.

Any items in body or query_params will be pre-populated in the confirmation form shown to the user.
Always populate these arguments where possible, either from the user's request or best inference.

name: infoblox-portal_make_put_request
description:
Execute a PUT request to create or replace objects.

Calling this tool will surface a UI confirmation form for the user with the ability
to edit fields before any changes are committed. Call this tool directly when the user asks to update
or modify something — you do not need to ask for permission or clarification first.

Any items in body or query_params will be pre-populated in the confirmation form shown to the user.
Always populate these arguments where possible, either from the user's request or best inference.
params: body:[{'additionalProperties': True, 'description': 'Body to include with the request', 'type': 'object'}, {'type': 'null'}]

name: infoblox-portal_make_patch_request
description:
Execute a PATCH request to update existing objects.

Calling this tool will surface a UI confirmation form for the user with the ability
to edit fields before any changes are committed. Call this tool directly when the user asks to update
or modify something — you do not need to ask for permission or clarification first.

Any items in body or query_params will be pre-populated in the confirmation form shown to the user.
Always populate these arguments where possible, either from the user's request or best inference.

name: infoblox-portal_make_delete_request
description:
Execute a DELETE request to remove objects.

Calling this tool will surface a UI confirmation form for the user with the ability
to edit fields before any changes are committed. Call this tool directly when the user asks to update
or modify something — you do not need to ask for permission or clarification first.

Any items in body or query_params will be pre-populated in the confirmation form shown to the user.
Always populate these arguments where possible, either from the user's request or best inference.

name: infoblox-portal_network_entity_search
description:
Search Infoblox/BloxOne entities (hosts, IPs, DNS records, assets, tags, users, etc.)
across the customer's environment using the Global Search API. Hits are only returned
for exact matches to the query.

Not for web/internet search or public external resources.

Returns a list of dicts containing metadata for matched entities.

name: infoblox-portal_list_all_cubes
description:
List all available CubeJS cubes and their descriptions.

Use this tool to discover what analytical data cubes are available.
Each cube represents a dataset that can be queried for insights.
Available cubes cover areas including: DNS (activity logs, metrics), DHCP (activity logs, metrics), Tokens (utilization only, capacity is in services), Insights/Actions (network, config, security, Infoblox IQ, assets), Assets (inventory, users, network interfaces, discovery provider details, insights).

When 5 or fewer cubes exist, full details (dimensions, measures, segments)
are automatically included. For more than 5 cubes, use `get_cube_info` to
get detailed information about a specific cube before querying.

Returns a list of cubes with:
- name: The cube identifier to use in queries
- title: Human-readable name
- description: What data the cube contains
- type: Whether it's a 'cube' or 'view'
- dimensions, measures, segments: (only when <= 5 cubes)

name: infoblox-portal_get_cube_info
description:
Get detailed metadata for a specific CubeJS cube.

Returns comprehensive information about the cube including:
- Dimensions: Fields you can group by or filter on (e.g., device_ip, timestamp)
- Measures: Aggregatable values (e.g., count, sum, average)
- Segments: Pre-defined filters that can be applied

Use this information to construct queries with the `query_cube` tool.

Example dimension types:
- string: Text values for filtering/grouping
- number: Numeric values
- time: Timestamps that support time-based analysis
- boolean: True/false values

Example measure types:
- count: Number of records
- sum: Total of numeric values
- avg: Average value
- min/max: Extreme values

name: infoblox-portal_query_cube
description:
Execute a CubeJS query and store results for SQL analysis.

This tool queries the specified cube with the given parameters and stores
the results in a Parquet file that can be queried with `query_stored_data`.

Query Building Tips:
1. Always use fully qualified names (CubeName.field) for measures and dimensions
2. Use get_cube_info first to see available fields
3. Time dimensions support relative ranges like '24 hours', '7 days' (auto-converted to date tuples)
4. Filters use operators like: equals, notEquals, contains, gt, gte, lt, lte

Common Patterns:
- Time series: Add time_dimensions with granularity for trends over time
- Top N: Use order with limit to get highest/lowest values
- Filtering: Use filters to narrow down to specific criteria
- Grouping: Add dimensions to segment data by categories

Returns:
    Table name and metadata for use with query_stored_data tool.

name: infoblox-portal_query_stored_data
description:
Execute a SQL query against stored API response data using DuckDB.

This tool allows you to:
- Query data from previous GET requests using standard SQL
- Join data across multiple API responses
- Filter, aggregate, and analyze data with full SQL capabilities

Table naming: Tables are named based on the service, method, and endpoint.
Use the table_name returned from make_get_request (e.g., 'ipamsvc_get_ipam_subnet.parquet')

DuckDB SQL Features:
- Standard SQL: SELECT, WHERE, JOIN, GROUP BY, ORDER BY, etc.
- Aggregations: COUNT(), SUM(), AVG(), MIN(), MAX()
- Window functions, CTEs, and more

Examples:
- Simple query: "SELECT * FROM ipamsvc_get_ipam_subnet.parquet WHERE address LIKE '10.%'"
- Nested field filtering: "SELECT * FROM dns_get_records.parquet WHERE data.type = 'A'"
- Join: "SELECT a.*, b.name FROM table1.parquet a JOIN table2.parquet b ON a.id = b.id"
- Aggregate: "SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count FROM ipamsvc_get_ipam_subnet.parquet GROUP BY status"
- List files: "SELECT * FROM glob('*.parquet')" to see available tables

name: infoblox-portal_generate_chart
description:
Generate a formatted A2UI chart component from a stored Parquet table.

The chart is registered in the ChartRegistry under a human-readable ID
(HRID) and can be rendered via 'ui://charts/{hrid}'.  Rehydration steps
(the upstream query pipeline that produced the table) are derived
automatically from audit artifacts — no manual specification required.
When the resource is accessed, any duckdb_query steps are re-run so the
chart always reflects the latest Parquet data.

When the source table has one row per entity rather than one row per
category, supply group_by_column, aggregation, and aggregation_alias to
pre-aggregate the data before charting.

name: infoblox-portal_user_action
description:
Handle A2UI user-action events.

The A2UI client calls this tool when a user interacts with an
action-bearing component (e.g. a form submit button).  Routing
is based on the ``name`` argument.

name: infoblox-portal_list_tools
description: Return the catalog of A2UI tools callable via call_tool.

name: infoblox-portal_call_tool
description:
Invoke an A2UI tool by name.

The A2UI client calls this tool to execute a dynamic UI action.
Use ``list_tools`` to discover available tool names and their schemas.

name: infoblox-portal_read_resource
description:
Read a resource by its URI.

For static resources, provide the exact URI. For templated
resources, provide the URI with template parameters filled in.

Returns the resource content as a string. Binary content is
base64-encoded.
